# Triage Dashboard — Shared Format & Rules

This file defines the shared dashboard template, data extraction rules, and
category mappings used by workflows that maintain the triage dashboard issue.

## Category Emoji Mapping

| Category | Emoji | Label |
|---|---|---|
| bug-report | 🐛 | bug |
| feature-request | ✨ | enhancement |
| duplicate | 🔁 | duplicate |
| discussion | 💬 | discussion |
| wrong-usage | ⚠️ | wrong-usage |
| unclear | ❓ | needs-info |

## Repro Status Display

| Value | Display |
|---|---|
| Yes | ✅ Yes |
| No | ❌ No |
| Failed | ⚠️ Failed |
| Skipped | ⏭️ Skipped |
| N/A | — |

## Triage Issue Body Parsing Rules

Each triage tracking issue body contains structured metadata at the top.
Extract data using these field patterns:

| Field | Pattern | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Upstream | `**Upstream:** <url>` | `**Upstream:** https://github.com/dotnet/machinelearning/issues/7522` |
| Status | `**Status:** <value>` | `**Status:** COMPLETE` |
| Classification | `**Classification:** <value>` | `**Classification:** bug-report` |
| Confidence | `**Confidence:** <value>` | `**Confidence:** 0.92` |
| Reproduced | `**Reproduced:** <value>` | `**Reproduced:** ✅ Yes` |
| Area | `**Area:** <value>` | `**Area:** AutoML` |

For the **Reproduced** field, normalize these variants:
- "✅ Yes", "Yes" → reproduced
- "❌ No", "No" → not reproduced
- "⚠️ Failed", "⚠️ Failed", "Failed" → repro-failed
- "⏭️ Skipped", "Skipped" → skipped

For determining if a **fix was suggested**, check for the presence of a
`## Suggested Fix` or `**Suggested Fix**` section in the body, OR
the `fix-suggested` label on the issue.

For the **upstream issue number**, extract from the URL in the Upstream field
(the number after `/issues/`), or from the issue title pattern
`[triage] upstream#<NUMBER>`.

## Dashboard Body Template

Use this exact template structure when generating or updating the dashboard
issue body. Replace all `<placeholder>` values with actual computed data.

```markdown
# 🔍 ML.NET Issue Triage Dashboard

> Last updated: <TIMESTAMP_ISO8601>
> Generated by: <WORKFLOW_NAME> workflow

## Summary Statistics

| Metric | Count |
|---|---|
| Total Investigated | <total_count> |
| Bug Reports | <bug_count> |
| Feature Requests | <feature_count> |
| Duplicates | <duplicate_count> |
| Questions/Discussion | <discussion_count> |
| Wrong Usage | <wrong_usage_count> |
| Unclear | <unclear_count> |
| **Reproduced** | **<reproduced_count>** |
| Repro Failed | <repro_failed_count> |
| Fix Suggested | <fix_suggested_count> |
| Pending (In Progress) | <pending_count> |

## Average Confidence Score: <avg_confidence>

## By Area

| Area | Bugs | Features | Other | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <area_name> | <bug_count> | <feature_count> | <other_count> | <total> |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

## All Investigations

| Upstream | Classification | Reproduced? | Fix? | Status | Fork Issue |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [#<N>](https://github.com/dotnet/machinelearning/issues/<N>) | <emoji> <category> | <repro_status> | <fix_status> | <status> | #<fork_issue_number> |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

## Action Items

### 🐛 Ready for Fix (reproduced + fix suggested)
- #<fork> — [upstream#<N>](https://github.com/dotnet/machinelearning/issues/<N>): <title> — **<complexity>**

### ❓ Needs Upstream Info
- #<fork> — <description>

### 📋 Low-Complexity Fixes
- #<fork> — <description>

### 🔁 Potential Duplicates to Verify
- #<fork> — <description>
```

### Important Notes

- The "All Investigations" table MUST include ALL triage issues, not just the
  last 10. Sort by fork issue number descending (newest first).
- Each row links to the upstream issue and references the fork tracking issue.
- The "Action Items" sections should be populated based on labels and parsed data:
  - **Ready for Fix**: issues with both `reproduced` and `fix-suggested` labels
  - **Needs Upstream Info**: issues classified as `unclear` or with `needs-info` label
  - **Low-Complexity Fixes**: issues with `fix-suggested` label where the body
    mentions "Low complexity" or "one-line fix" or similar
  - **Potential Duplicates**: issues classified as `duplicate`
- If a section has no items, show "- No items at this time."
